title: Software Repair — CI/test failure, bounded verified patch
required_repository_authorization: read+write to a customer-authorized repo, or a bundled local fixture; NOOS never touches a repo it was not explicitly given
not_supported_pilot_note: Pilot is narrow: Python(pytest) and Node(node:test) only. NO claim of universal repository or language support.
network_policy: worker: no outbound network except the configured model provider endpoint; repair patches may not add network calls to forbidden hosts
test_discovery_policy: explicit test command from the job spec wins; otherwise pytest -q (python) / node --test (node)
branch_policy: all changes on an isolated branch noos/repair/<job_id>; base branch and main are never modified in place
draft_pr_policy: open a DRAFT pull request only when the job spec grants pr:write and open_pr=true; otherwise emit a downloadable patch bundle

output_contract.verified_patch: unified diff constrained to allowed paths + limits
output_contract.verification_evidence: tests-before (failing) and tests-after (passing) captured output + exit codes
output_contract.customer_report: root cause, changed files, test evidence, provenance, cost
output_contract.delivery: draft PR url OR patch bundle path

provenance.receipt_origin_for_repair_execution: local_reference (pilot runner) or organic (only from the production canonical runner)
provenance.note: A repair JOB succeeding is a customer outcome; it is NOT infrastructure liveness (see production_running_confirmed).
